denial_pattern_sources: appeal_closed_as_withdrawn: State Insurance Commissioner complaint logs (CA DOI 2022, NY DFS 2023)
contraindication_to_step_therapy: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; State step-therapy exception laws (NY, TX, IL)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 10/20/2026 for Partial nail matrixectomy related to Ingrown toenail, recurrent (L60.0). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Partial nail matrixectomy is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Ingrown toenail, recurrent (L60.0). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Partial nail matrixectomy recommended as the next appropriate step. Three infections in 12 months; failed conservative podiatry care. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
